@@ -427,7 +427,7 @@
 {"id":"brenner_bot-ivx","title":"Fix artifact merge: ignore payload id/kill fields","description":"## Problem
The artifact merge algorithm currently allows delta payloads to overwrite system-owned fields like `id` and kill metadata (e.g. `killed`, `killed_by`, `killed_at`, `kill_reason`).

### Why this matters
Per `specs/artifact_delta_spec_v0.1.md`, IDs are assigned by the merge for `ADD` operations ("target_id MUST be null (ID will be assigned by merge)"). Allowing a payload to set or mutate `id` breaks determinism + referential integrity and can create collisions or confusing artifacts.
Similarly, kill fields should only be mutated by the `KILL` operation (audit trail / semantics).

## Root cause
In `apps/web/src/lib/artifact-merge.ts`:
- `applyAdd` builds new items as `{ id: newId, ...payload }`, so payload can override `id`.
- Research thread creation does `{ id: "RT", ..., ...payload }`, so payload can override `id`.
- `applyEdit` loops over payload keys and will write `id` / kill fields directly onto existing items.

## Fix
- In `ADD`: explicitly ignore/strip `id` and kill fields from payload; always set `id` after payload.
- In `EDIT`: ignore attempts to edit `id` or kill metadata.
- Add focused unit tests proving payload cannot override `id` (ADD + EDIT + research_thread creation).

## Done when
- New tests pass and prevent regressions.
- `apps/web` unit tests + build pass.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-iyo","title":"Contact Web of Stories for transcript permission","description":"# Goal
Contact Web of Stories to request permission to host the Sydney Brenner transcript text.

Status: This is a human task (requires sending an email and tracking the reply).

---

# Why
We have a working policy decision that full transcripts should not be publicly hosted without explicit permission.

---

# Email template (copy/paste)
Subject: Permission request to host Sydney Brenner Web of Stories transcript text

Hello Web of Stories team,

I’m building a research project called “Brenner Bot” that studies the scientific method of Sydney Brenner using the Web of Stories interview transcripts.

I’d like to ask for permission to host the transcript text for the Sydney Brenner interview on a website (brennerbot.org) and in a public Git repository, with clear attribution and links back to the original videos/pages.

The purpose is educational and research-oriented: to build tools that help people (and AI assistants) learn and apply Brenner’s approach to hypothesis formation and discriminative experiment design.

If you’re open to this, could you please confirm:
1) Whether we may host the full transcript text publicly.
2) Any required attribution language, backlinks, or copyright notices.
3) Any restrictions (non-commercial only, excerpts only, etc.).

If full hosting is not permitted, we can restrict to short excerpts/quotes and link back to Web of Stories.

Thank you for your time, and thank you for preserving these interviews.

Best,
<YOUR NAME>
<OPTIONAL CONTACT>

---

# Acceptance
- Permission response received with a clear go/no-go decision (and any conditions).

 {"id":"brenner_bot-jabd","title":"Task: Session Lifecycle E2E Tests","description":"# Goal
Create E2E tests for the complete session lifecycle from creation to artifact viewing.

# User Journey to Test

## Session Creation Flow
1. Navigate to /sessions/new
2. Authenticate (if required)
3. Enter research question
4. Select excerpts from corpus
5. Choose Brenner operators (optional)
6. Configure session options
7. Submit session
8. See confirmation/redirect

## Session Monitoring Flow
1. View session list
2. Select active session
3. See status updates
4. View agent contributions
5. See progress indicators
6. Handle session completion

## Artifact Viewing Flow
1. Navigate to completed session
2. View compiled artifact
3. See hypothesis slate
4. See test designs
5. See adversarial critiques
6. Export/share artifact

# Test File Structure
```typescript
// e2e/session-lifecycle.spec.ts
import { test, expect } from './utils/test-fixtures';

test.describe('Session Lifecycle', () => {
  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    // Ensure authenticated state
  });
  
  test('creates new session with research question', async ({ page, logger }) => {
    await navigateTo(page, logger, '/sessions/new');
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Enter research question', async () => {
      await page.getByLabel(/research question/i).fill(
        'Does role-separated prompting improve artifact quality?'
      );
    });
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Select excerpts', async () => {
      await page.getByRole('button', { name: /select excerpts/i }).click();
      await page.getByText('§103').click();
      await page.getByText('§105').click();
      await page.getByRole('button', { name: /confirm/i }).click();
      
      logger.debug('Selected excerpts: §103, §105');
    });
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Submit session', async () => {
      await page.getByRole('button', { name: /start session/i }).click();
    });
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Verify redirect to session view', async () => {
      await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/sessions\/[a-z0-9-]+/);
      logger.info('Session created, redirected to session view');
    });
  });
  
  test('views session status and agent contributions', async ({ page, logger }) => {
    // Navigate to existing session (use fixture or create in beforeEach)
    await navigateTo(page, logger, '/sessions/test-session-id');
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Verify status display', async () => {
      await expect(page.getByText(/status/i)).toBeVisible();
      await expect(page.getByText(/(active|pending|complete)/i)).toBeVisible();
    });
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Check for agent contributions', async () => {
      const contributions = page.locator('[data-testid="agent-contribution"]');
      const count = await contributions.count();
      logger.info(`Found ${count} agent contributions`);
    });
  });
  
  test('views completed artifact with all sections', async ({ page, logger }) => {
    await navigateTo(page, logger, '/sessions/completed-session-id/artifact');
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Verify artifact sections', async () => {
      await expect(page.getByText(/research thread/i)).toBeVisible();
      await expect(page.getByText(/hypothesis slate/i)).toBeVisible();
      await expect(page.getByText(/discriminative tests/i)).toBeVisible();
      await expect(page.getByText(/adversarial critique/i)).toBeVisible();
      
      logger.info('All artifact sections present');
    });
  });
});
```

# Test Data Requirements
- Need fixture for completed session with valid artifact
- Need fixture for active session with partial contributions
- Need authenticated user state

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session creation flow tested end-to-end
- [ ] Form validation tested
- [ ] Session status monitoring tested
- [ ] Artifact viewing tested
- [ ] All required auth flows handled
- [ ] Loading states verified
- [ ] Error states handled gracefully","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenSnow","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:52:31.666347021-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:46:28.186447146-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:46:28.186447146-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive E2E tests for session lifecycle: 20 tests covering session list, detail, artifact viewing, and navigation flows. All tests pass on Chrome/Firefox.","labels":["testing e2e sessions critical"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-jabd","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-hvi7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:36:46.519034241-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","title":"EPIC: Research Intelligence System (Hypothesis Lifecycle + Method Feedback)","description":"# EPIC: Research Intelligence System

## The Gap This Fills

The Brenner method is fundamentally about **killing hypotheses** — treating them as "mistresses to be discarded once the pleasure is over" (§229). The system produces artifacts with hypothesis slates, discriminative tests, assumption ledgers, anomaly registers, and adversarial critiques. But there's no infrastructure for:

1. **Tracking hypothesis lifecycle** — A hypothesis born in Session 1 might be refined in Session 3 and killed in Session 7. Currently, hypotheses exist only as disconnected items in artifact files.

2. **Tracking assumptions** — Scale checks and background assumptions are critical but not first-class entities. When an assumption is falsified, which hypotheses are affected?

3. **Tracking anomalies** — Paradoxes and surprising observations drive the method (§103: "Both could be wrong"), but they exist only as text.

4. **Tracking critiques** — The Adversarial Critic role produces attacks, but there's no way to track which critiques have been addressed.

5. **Closing the research loop** — Tests are designed but not formally connected to their execution or results.

6. **Method feedback** — The evaluation_rubric_v0.1.md defines 14 scoring criteria, but there's no implementation.

## The Six Registries

This epic builds **four first-class registries** that map to artifact schema sections:

### 1. Hypothesis Registry
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 2: Hypothesis Slate
- Stable IDs (H-xxx), lifecycle states, cross-session linking
- Fields: statement, mechanism, confidence, category, anchors, linkedAssumptions, linkedAnomalies

### 2. Assumption Registry (NEW)
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 5: Assumption Ledger
- Types: background, methodological, boundary, scale_physics
- States: unchecked → challenged → verified/falsified
- **Key**: Every program MUST have at least one scale_physics assumption (§58: imprisoned imagination)
- Falsification cascades to dependent hypotheses and tests

### 3. Anomaly Registry (NEW)
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 6: Anomaly Register
- Quarantine status: active → resolved/deferred/paradigm_shifting
- Conflict tracking: which hypotheses/assumptions does this challenge?
- **Key**: Anomalies can SPAWN hypotheses (paradox grounding)

### 4. Critique Registry (NEW)
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 7: Adversarial Critique
- Target types: hypothesis, test, assumption, framing
- Response workflow: active → addressed/dismissed/accepted
- **Key**: Unaddressed critiques weaken targets; dashboard shows "hypotheses under attack"

### 5. Prediction Registry (via Test Binding)
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 3: Predictions Table
- First-class predictions separate from tests
- Per-hypothesis expected outcomes

### 6. Test Registry (via Test Binding)
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 4: Discriminative Tests
- **MANDATORY**: Potency checks (distinguishes "no effect" from "assay failed")
- Evidence-per-week scoring (4 dimensions, 0-12 total)
- Test→Prediction→Hypothesis binding

## Scorecard: 14-Criterion Evaluation

The Brenner Method Scorecard aligns with evaluation_rubric_v0.1.md:

**Universal (all roles):**
1. Structural Correctness (0-3)
2. Citation Compliance (0-3)
3. Rationale Quality (0-3)

**Hypothesis Generator (Codex):**
4. Level Separation (0-3, weight 1.5)
5. Third Alternative Presence (0-3, weight 2.0)
6. Paradox Exploitation (0-2, optional)

**Test Designer (Opus):**
7. Discriminative Power (0-3, weight 2.0)
8. Potency Check Sufficiency (0-3, weight 2.0)
9. Object Transposition Considered (0-2, optional)
10. Score Calibration Honesty (0-2)

**Adversarial Critic (Gemini):**
11. Scale Check Rigor (0-3, weight 1.5)
12. Anomaly Quarantine Discipline (0-3, weight 1.5)
13. Theory Kill Justification (0-3, when KILL, weight 1.5)
14. Real Third Alternative (0-3, weight 1.5)

## Research Program Dashboard

Aggregates all registries into program-level view:
- Hypothesis funnel (by state and origin type)
- Assumption health (scale checks, falsifications)
- Anomaly status (unresolved puzzles)
- Critique pressure (hypotheses under attack)
- Test execution (potency coverage, evidence scores)
- Timeline of all major events

## Feature Dependency Structure

```
Hypothesis Registry (foundation) ──────────────────┐
        ↓                                          │
Hypothesis Lifecycle States ───────────────────────┤
        ↓                                          │
Test→Hypothesis Binding (with Predictions)         │
        ↓                                          │
Assumption Registry ───────────────────────────────┤
        ↓                                          │
Anomaly Registry ──────────────────────────────────┤
        ↓                                          │
Critique Registry ─────────────────────────────────┤
        ↓                                          │
Brenner Method Scorecard ←─────────────────────────┘
        ↓                         (all registries feed)
Research Program Dashboard
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Hypotheses have stable IDs with full metadata (mechanism, anchors, etc.)
- [ ] Hypothesis lifecycle states tracked with transition history
- [ ] Test→Prediction→Hypothesis binding with potency checks
- [ ] Assumption registry with scale_physics enforcement
- [ ] Anomaly registry with hypothesis spawning
- [ ] Critique registry with response workflow
- [ ] 14-criterion scorecard computed per session
- [ ] Program dashboard aggregates all registries

## Non-Goals

- Not predictive analytics (what will be killed next)
- Not automated test execution
- Not replacing human judgment on method quality","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:37:32.539527383-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:43:36.732262159-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:35:50.697324851-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-juv","title":"EPIC: Session Interface & Excerpt Builder","description":"# Purpose

Enable users to run Brenner Loop research sessions via Agent Mail. This is the 'lab' functionality - where the method becomes executable.

# Pages

1. **/sessions** - List of sessions (active, completed) with status
2. **/sessions/new** - Create new session with excerpt builder
3. **/sessions/[id]** - Session detail view with timeline, artifacts

# Components Needed

- **SessionCard** - Card showing session status, agents, thread ID
- **SessionTimeline** - Visual timeline of messages, agent responses
- **ExcerptBuilder** - Select text from documents, build cited excerpt blocks
- **ArtifactViewer** - Display hypothesis slates, tests, ledgers
- **AgentBadge** - Color-coded badge for Claude/GPT/Gemini
- **StatusIndicator** - Visual indicator for session status

# Excerpt Builder Details

The excerpt builder is critical - it's how users compose the context for their sessions:

1. Navigate to a document, select text
2. Selection shows up in 'excerpt basket' with §-anchor
3. User can reorder, add comments
4. Final excerpt block is formatted for inclusion in kickoff prompt

# Technical Decisions

- Excerpt selections stored in client state (localStorage for persistence)
- Session list fetches from Agent Mail API
- Session detail polls for updates or uses WebSocket
- Artifacts rendered as structured markdown

# Lab Mode Gating

Session creation should require auth (lab mode). Public visitors can view existing sessions but not create new ones.

# Depends On

- Design System Foundation
- Corpus Browser (for excerpt selection in documents)
- Landing Page (for navigation)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:12:14.885500044-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:17.966380606-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:17.966380606-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.2 (Excerpt builder) + brenner_bot-5so.4.3 (Sessions UI) + brenner_bot-5so.4.4 (Lab gating)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-juv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1qx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:12:25.471699138-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-juv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-e00","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:12:25.531616279-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","title":"Feature: Test→Hypothesis Binding","description":"# Feature: Test→Hypothesis Binding (ENHANCED)

## Purpose
Close the research loop by formally connecting test execution to hypothesis state changes. **CRITICAL UPDATES**: Predictions are now first-class entities, and tests MUST have potency checks.

## CRITICAL UPDATES from Review

After reviewing the artifact schema and evaluation rubric:

### Issue 1: Predictions Must Be First-Class
The artifact schema separates **Predictions Table (Section 3)** from **Discriminative Tests (Section 4)**:
- A **Prediction** is: "Under condition X, H1 predicts Y, H2 predicts Z"
- A **Test** is: "Do X and observe the result"

These are DISTINCT because:
- Multiple tests might address the same prediction
- A prediction might be tested multiple times with different methods

### Issue 2: Potency Checks Are MANDATORY
From evaluation rubric "Potency Check Sufficiency" (0-3):
- 0: No potency check included → **AUTOMATIC FAILURE**
- 3: Complete potency check: positive control, sensitivity verification, timing validation

From artifact schema: "Each test MUST include a potency check"

### Issue 3: Tests Need Evidence-Per-Week Scoring
The artifact schema requires tests to have a 12-point score across 4 dimensions:
- Likelihood ratio (0-3): How discriminative?
- Cost (0-3): How expensive?
- Speed (0-3): How fast?
- Ambiguity (0-3): How clear is the readout?

### Issue 4: Object Transposition
From evaluation rubric "Object Transposition Considered" (0-2):
- Has the contributor considered alternative experimental systems?

## Enhanced Schemas

### Prediction Schema (NEW - First-Class Entity)
```typescript
export const PredictionSchema = z.object({
  /** Stable ID: P-{session_id}-{sequence} */
  id: z.string().regex(/^P-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/),
  
  /** The observable condition */
  condition: z.string().min(10),
  
  /** What each hypothesis predicts for this condition */
  hypothesisPredictions: z.array(z.object({
    hypothesisId: z.string(),
    prediction: z.string(),
    rationale: z.string().optional(),
  })).min(2),  // Must discriminate at least 2 hypotheses
  
  /** Session where prediction was formulated */
  sessionId: z.string(),
  
  /** Which tests address this prediction? */
  linkedTests: z.array(z.string()).optional(),  // T-xxx IDs
  
  createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
});
```

### Enhanced Test Schema
```typescript
export const PotencyCheckSchema = z.object({
  /** Positive control that would show the effect if present */
  positiveControl: z.string(),
  
  /** How do we verify detection threshold is adequate? */
  sensitivityVerification: z.string().optional(),
  
  /** How do we know the assay window is correct? */
  timingValidation: z.string().optional(),
});

export const EvidencePerWeekScoreSchema = z.object({
  likelihoodRatio: z.number().min(0).max(3),  // 0=<2:1, 1=2-10:1, 2=10-100:1, 3=>100:1
  cost: z.number().min(0).max(3),              // 0=>$100K, 1=$10K-$100K, 2=$1K-$10K, 3=<$1K
  speed: z.number().min(0).max(3),             // 0=>1yr, 1=1-6mo, 2=1wk-1mo, 3=<1wk
  ambiguity: z.number().min(0).max(3),         // 0=many confounds, 3=digital readout
  total: z.number().min(0).max(12),
});

export const TestRecordSchema = z.object({
  /** Stable ID: T-{session_id}-{sequence} */
  id: z.string().regex(/^T-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/),
  
  /** Test name for display */
  name: z.string(),
  
  /** Full procedure description */
  procedure: z.string().min(20),
  
  /** Which hypotheses does this test discriminate? */
  discriminates: z.array(z.string()).min(2),  // H-xxx IDs
  
  /** Expected outcomes per hypothesis */
  expectedOutcomes: z.array(z.object({
    hypothesisId: z.string(),
    outcome: z.string(),
  })),
  
  /** Which predictions does this test address? */
  addressesPredictions: z.array(z.string()).optional(),  // P-xxx IDs
  
  /** MANDATORY: Potency check */
  potencyCheck: PotencyCheckSchema,  // NOT optional!
  
  /** 4-dimension scoring */
  evidencePerWeekScore: EvidencePerWeekScoreSchema,
  
  /** Object transposition notes (alternative experimental systems) */
  objectTransposition: z.object({
    considered: z.boolean(),
    alternatives: z.array(z.object({
      system: z.string(),
      pros: z.string(),
      cons: z.string(),
    })).optional(),
    chosenRationale: z.string().optional(),
  }).optional(),
  
  /** Feasibility assessment */
  feasibility: z.object({
    requirements: z.string(),
    difficulty: z.enum(['easy', 'moderate', 'hard', 'very_hard']),
    blockers: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
  }),
  
  /** Which assumptions does this test require? */
  requiredAssumptions: z.array(z.string()).optional(),  // A-xxx IDs
  
  /** Session where test was designed */
  designedInSession: z.string(),
  
  /** Test status */
  status: z.enum(['designed', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'blocked']),
  
  /** If completed, execution record */
  execution: TestExecutionSchema.optional(),
  
  createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
  updatedAt: z.string().datetime(),
});
```

## Validation Rules

From evaluation rubric:

**Discriminative Power (0-3):**
- Test MUST have different expected outcomes for each hypothesis
- Outcomes should be binary or quantitatively large differences
- Both outcomes must be actually observable

**Potency Check Sufficiency (0-3):**
- AUTOMATIC FAILURE if no potency check
- Must distinguish "no effect" from "assay failed"

**Score Calibration Honesty (0-2):**
- Scores should be conservative, not all 3s

## CLI Commands

### Prediction Commands (NEW)
- `brenner prediction list`
- `brenner prediction show <id>`
- `brenner prediction create --condition="..." --h1-predicts="..." --h2-predicts="..."`

### Test Commands (Enhanced)
- `brenner test create` — Now REQUIRES potency check, scores
- `brenner test validate <id>` — Check test meets all requirements
- `brenner test execute <id>` — Record execution (unchanged)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Predictions are first-class entities with own schema/storage
- [ ] Tests MUST have potency checks (validation error if missing)
- [ ] Tests have 4-dimension evidence-per-week scores
- [ ] Object transposition tracking available
- [ ] Test validation warns on inflated scores
- [ ] Links between predictions, tests, and hypotheses

## Related Specs
- specs/artifact_schema_v0.1.md — Sections 3, 4
- specs/evaluation_rubric_v0.1.md — Criteria 7, 8, 9, 10","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:41:43.843308752-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:29:03.745723691-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:29:03.745723691-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Prediction schema as first-class entity and TestRecord schema with mandatory potency checks, evidence-per-week scoring, and discriminative power validation.  {"id":"brenner_bot-mhi","title":"TanStack Store: Create Compare View State Persistence","description":"# Goal
Persist the distillation comparison view state so users don't lose their selections.

## Background
The CompareView component has UI state that should persist:
- leftModel: Which model is in left pane (opus/gpt/gemini)
- rightModel: Which model is in right pane
- syncScroll: Whether panes scroll together
- Per-model scroll positions

Currently, refreshing the page resets all this state.

## Store Schema
```typescript
interface CompareStore {
  leftModel: 'opus' | 'gpt' | 'gemini';
  rightModel: 'opus' | 'gpt' | 'gemini';
  syncScroll: boolean;
  scrollPositions: Record<string, number>; // model -> scrollY
}
```

## Implementation
```typescript
import { Store } from '@tanstack/store';

const compareStore = new Store<CompareStore>({
  leftModel: 'opus',
  rightModel: 'gpt',
  syncScroll: true,
  scrollPositions: {},
});

// Persist to localStorage
compareStore.subscribe(() => {
  localStorage.setItem('brenner-compare', JSON.stringify(compareStore.state));
});

// React hook
export function useCompareState() {
  return useStore(compareStore, (s) => s);
}

export function setLeftModel(model: Model) {
  compareStore.setState((s) => ({ ...s, leftModel: model }));
}
```

## Integration
```typescript
function CompareView() {
  const { leftModel, rightModel, syncScroll } = useCompareState();
  
  return (
    <div>
      <ModelSelector 
        value={leftModel} 
        onChange={setLeftModel}
        exclude={rightModel}
      />
      {/* ... */}
    </div>
  );
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Model selections persist
- [ ] Sync scroll preference persists
- [ ] Per-model scroll positions persist
- [ ] State restores on page load

## Files to Create
- apps/web/src/stores/compareStore.ts
- apps/web/src/hooks/useCompareState.ts

## Files to Modify

-{"id":"brenner_bot-mqg7","title":"Feature: Operator Intervention Audit Trail","description":"# Goal
Implement a formal audit trail for human operator interventions during Brenner Loop sessions, enabling reproducibility, trust verification, and learning from overrides.

## The Gap This Fills

Other agents covered:
- Human-in-the-loop gates (general concept)
- Cockpit runner (orchestration)
- Web UI for sessions

**What nobody covered:** When a human operator overrides an agent decision (e.g., manually killing a hypothesis, forcing a test to be included, editing an artifact before publish), there is no formal record of:
- WHAT was changed
- WHO made the change  
- WHY they made it (rationale)
- WHEN it happened relative to agent activities

This matters because:
1. **Reproducibility** - Can someone else replicate this research if they dont know a human secretly tweaked the artifact?
2. **Trust** - Was this conclusion reached by agents following the protocol, or did a human push it through?
3. **Learning** - If operators frequently override a certain agent/role, maybe the role prompt needs improvement.
4. **Debugging** - When a session goes wrong, was it agent error or operator error?

## Why This Is Different from Deltas

Agent contributions are recorded as DELTA messages with `from: AgentName`. But operator interventions can happen in several ways that bypass the delta flow:

1. **Direct artifact editing** before publish (editing the markdown)
2. **Selective delta exclusion** (compiling without some deltas)
3. **Manual message injection** (posting a DELTA on behalf of an agent)
4. **Override decisions** ("ignore this critique", "force-include this test")
5. **Session control actions** (early termination, forced re-run, role reassignment)

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Operator Intervention Record

```typescript
interface OperatorIntervention {
  id: string;
  session_id: string;
  timestamp: string;
  operator_id: string;  // From BRENNER_AGENT_NAME or "human"
  
  type: 
    | "artifact_edit"      // Direct edit to compiled artifact
    | "delta_exclusion"    // Excluded a delta from compilation
    | "delta_injection"    // Added a delta not from an agent
    | "decision_override"  // Overrode a protocol decision
    | "session_control"    // Terminated, forked, or reset session
    | "role_reassignment"; // Changed agent-role mappings mid-session
  
  target: {
    message_id?: number;   // For delta exclusion/injection
    artifact_version?: number;
    item_id?: string;      // Hypothesis, test, etc.
  };
  
  before?: string;  // State before intervention (for diffs)
  after?: string;   // State after intervention
  
  rationale: string;  // Required: WHY did the operator do this?
  
  reversible: boolean;
  reversed_at?: string;
  reversed_by?: string;
}
```

### 2. Intervention Capture Points

| Action | Capture Point | Implementation |
|--------|---------------|----------------|
| Edit artifact before publish | Compare pre/post in publish action | API route adds intervention record |
| Exclude delta | Compile action with exclusion list | Track excluded message_ids |
| Manual delta | DELTA message where from = operator | Check if sender matches known agent roster |
| Early termination | Session close action | Add "session_control" record |
| Role reassignment | Roster change mid-session | Compare before/after roster state |

### 3. Storage and Access

**Storage:** Interventions stored as part of session metadata in Agent Mail thread (as INFO: messages with structured payload) or in a separate interventions log.

**Access:**
- `brenner session interventions <threadId>` - List all interventions
- Session detail page shows intervention count and expandable list
- Compiled artifacts include intervention summary in metadata

### 4. Intervention Severity Levels

```
MINOR: Typo fixes, formatting adjustments
MODERATE: Delta exclusion, small edits
MAJOR: Killing hypotheses, adding tests, role changes
CRITICAL: Session termination, protocol bypass
```

Major/Critical interventions trigger warnings in the UI.

### 5. Reproducibility Mode

A "strict" compilation mode that:
- Fails if any MAJOR+ interventions exist
- Produces a "clean" artifact that is purely agent-derived
- Useful for comparing "what would agents have produced alone?"

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `OperatorIntervention` type defined in session types
- [ ] Publish action detects and records artifact edits
- [ ] Compile action accepts `excludeDeltas` param and records exclusions
- [ ] Manual delta detection (sender not in agent roster)
- [ ] CLI `brenner session interventions <threadId>` command
- [ ] Web session detail shows intervention badge/list
- [ ] All interventions require `rationale` field
- [ ] Compiled artifact metadata includes `intervention_count`

## Dependencies

- Agent roster (brenner_bot-5so.12.1.x) - needed to detect "operator vs agent"
- Session compile/publish actions (already exist)

## Non-Goals

- Not preventing interventions (operators should have full control)
- Not implementing approval workflows (thats for future governance)
- Not tracking every click/action (only meaningful interventions)

## Background: Why This Matters for Science

Real science has lab notebooks that record everything, including mistakes and corrections. The audit trail is the digital equivalent. Without it, a "research result" from a Brenner Loop session cannot be trusted or replicated by others.

The Brenner Method emphasizes transparency and honesty about what was done and why. Occams Broom (hiding inconvenient facts) is an anti-pattern. The audit trail ensures operator interventions are visible, not swept under the rug.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"GoldFalcon","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:31.405372314-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T15:02:15.667567682-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mqg7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:38:22.90910216-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

- Not tracking every click/action (only meaningful interventions)

## Background: Why This Matters for Science

Real science has lab notebooks that record everything, including mistakes and corrections. The audit trail is the digital equivalent. Without it, a "research result" from a Brenner Loop session cannot be trusted or replicated by others.

The Brenner Method emphasizes transparency and honesty about what was done and why. Occams Broom (hiding inconvenient facts) is an anti-pattern.  {"id":"brenner_bot-nihv","title":"Feature: Brenner Method Scorecard","description":"# Feature: Brenner Method Scorecard (COMPREHENSIVE REWRITE)

## Purpose
Implement the evaluation rubric as automated tooling. Provides per-session and per-contribution scores on Brenner method adherence with actionable feedback.

## CRITICAL REWRITE

The original 7-dimension design was **too simplified**. The evaluation rubric has **14 specific criteria** organized by role. The scorecard must align with the actual rubric.

## Rubric Alignment

### Universal Criteria (All Contributions)
| # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures |
|---|-----------|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Structural Correctness | 0-3 | Delta format compliance |
| 2 | Citation Compliance | 0-3 | §n anchors, [inference] markers |
| 3 | Rationale Quality | 0-3 | Explains why, which operators |

### Hypothesis Generator (Codex) Criteria
| # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures |
|---|-----------|--------|------------------|
| 4 | Level Separation | 0-3 | ⊘ operator, no conflation |
| 5 | Third Alternative Presence | 0-3 | Genuine third option |
| 6 | Paradox Exploitation | 0-2 | Uses anomalies productively |

### Test Designer (Opus) Criteria
| # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures |
|---|-----------|--------|------------------|
| 7 | Discriminative Power | 0-3 | Tests distinguish hypotheses |
| 8 | Potency Check Sufficiency | 0-3 | Can detect assay failure |
| 9 | Object Transposition | 0-2 | Alternative systems considered |
| 10 | Score Calibration Honesty | 0-2 | Conservative, not inflated |

### Adversarial Critic (Gemini) Criteria
| # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures |
|---|-----------|--------|------------------|
| 11 | Scale Check Rigor | 0-3 | Physics calculations, not hand-waves |
| 12 | Anomaly Quarantine Discipline | 0-3 | Tracking, not sweeping |
| 13 | Theory Kill Justification | 0-3 | Kills are evidenced |
| 14 | Real Third Alternative | 0-3 | Constructive, not just skeptical |

## Data Sources

Now that we have all registries, each dimension has a proper data source:

| Criterion | Data Source |
|-----------|-------------|
| Citation Compliance | Hypothesis.anchors, Test.anchors (from registries) |
| Level Separation | Hypothesis.mechanism validation |
| Third Alternative Presence | Count of hypotheses with category='third_alternative' |
| Paradox Exploitation | Anomaly Registry: anomalies that spawned hypotheses |
| Discriminative Power | Test predictions: are outcomes different? |
| Potency Check Sufficiency | Test.potencyCheck presence and completeness |
| Scale Check Rigor | Assumption Registry: count and quality of scale_physics assumptions |
| Anomaly Quarantine Discipline | Anomaly Registry: status tracking |
| Theory Kill Justification | Critique→Kill linkage in transitions |
| Real Third Alternative | Critique.proposedAlternative with testable=true |

## Per-Contribution Scoring

From rubric, weighted composite per role:

**Hypothesis Generator (Codex):**
```
Score = (Structural × 1.0) + (Citation × 1.0) + (Rationale × 0.5) +
        (Level-Sep × 1.5) + (Third-Alt × 2.0) + (Paradox × 0.5)
Max = 19 points
```

**Test Designer (Opus):**
```
Score = (Structural × 1.0) + (Citation × 1.0) + (Rationale × 0.5) +
        (Discriminative × 2.0) + (Potency × 2.0) + (Object-Trans × 0.5) + (Calibration × 0.5)
Max = 21.5 points
```

**Adversarial Critic (Gemini):**
```
Score = (Structural × 1.0) + (Citation × 1.0) + (Rationale × 0.5) +
        (Scale × 1.5) + (Quarantine × 1.5) + [Kill-Justify × 1.5]* + (Real-Third × 1.5)
Max (with KILL) = 25.5 points
Max (ADD/EDIT) = 21 points
```

## Session-Level Aggregation

For evaluating a complete session:

1. **Volume**: Count of valid contributions per role
2. **Quality**: Mean composite score per role
3. **Progression**: Did quality improve over rounds?
4. **Convergence**: Did hypotheses narrow (kills > adds by end)?
5. **Coverage**: Were all operators in the role's toolkit used?

## Pass/Fail Gates

Certain failures are automatic disqualifiers:

| Failure | Consequence |
|---------|-------------|
| Invalid JSON in delta | Delta rejected |
| Missing potency check | Test rejected |
| Fake transcript anchor | Contribution flagged |
| KILL without rationale | KILL rejected |
| Third alternative missing | Hypothesis set incomplete |

## Warning Thresholds

| Condition | Warning |
|-----------|---------|
| Composite < 50% of max | Low-quality contribution |
| > 3 ADDs without KILL | Possible hypothesis sprawl |
| Scale check missing for mechanism claim | Unverified claim |
| Potency score < 2 | Weak assay design |

## Feedback Generation

For each low-scoring dimension, generate specific feedback:

**Example for Level Separation (scored 1/3):**
```
[WARNING] Level Separation (1/3)
  Issue: Hypothesis mechanism shows level conflation
  Found: "The gene tells the cell to differentiate" (H-RS20251230-001)
  Problem: This anthropomorphizes genes with agency
  
  Suggestion: Reframe as mechanism: "Gene X expression enables 
  transcription of differentiation factors"
  
  Sydney Brenner says: "Programs don't have wants. Interpreters do." (§58)
```

## CLI Commands

- `brenner score <session-id>` — Score session with all 14 criteria
- `brenner score contribution <delta-id>` — Score single contribution
- `brenner feedback <session-id>` — Detailed feedback per dimension
- `brenner validate <session-id>` — Check pass/fail gates only
- `brenner score --program=<id>` — Aggregate across program sessions

## Integration Points

- **Hypothesis Registry**: Validates mechanism, anchors, category
- **Assumption Registry**: Validates scale_physics presence/quality
- **Anomaly Registry**: Validates quarantine discipline
- **Critique Registry**: Validates kill justification
- **Test Binding**: Validates discriminative power, potency

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 14 criteria from rubric implemented
- [ ] Per-role weighted composites match rubric
- [ ] Data sources connected to registries
- [ ] Pass/fail gates enforced
- [ ] Warning thresholds generate alerts
- [ ] Brenner quotes integrated into feedback
- [ ] Session-level aggregation working
